primary flows:   1. executeAndCreate \u2013 run arbitrary calls (e.g. DEX swap), then lock the      resulting token balance in an HTLC.   2. redeemAndExecute \u2013 redeem tokens from an HTLC via EIP-712 signature,      run arbitrary calls (e.g. DEX swap), then sweep the result to the caller.      Front-running safe: the HTLC-level signature binds to this coordinator as      msg.sender, and the coordinator verifies claimAddress == msg.sender.   3. refundAndExecute \u2013 refund an expired HTLC (created via this coordinator),      run arbitrary calls (e.g. swap WBTC back to USDC), then sweep to the      original depositor.","errors":{"SafeERC20FailedOperation(address)":[{"details":"An operation with an ERC-20 token failed."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"collabRefundAndExecute(bytes32,uint256,address,address,uint256,(address,uint256,bytes)[],address,uint256,uint8,bytes32,bytes32,uint8,bytes32,bytes32)":{"details":"Requires TWO signatures:      - Depositor EIP-712 sig (coordinator-level, authorizes refund params)      - ClaimAddress EIP-712 sig (HTLC-level, waives timelock via refundBySig)      Anyone can submit the transaction (gasless for both parties).","params":{"amount":"Token amount locked in the HTLC","calls":"Arbitrary calls to execute after refund (e.g. fee skim + DEX swap)","claimAddress":"Claim address set at HTLC creation","claimR":"ECDSA signature component (claimAddress's HTLC-level refundBySig signature)","claimS":"ECDSA signature component (claimAddress's HTLC-level refundBySig signature)","claimV":"ECDSA recovery id (claimAddress's HTLC-level refundBySig signature)","depositorR":"ECDSA signature component (depositor's coordinator-level signature)","depositorS":"ECDSA signature component (depositor's coordinator-level signature)","depositorV":"ECDSA recovery id (depositor's coordinator-level signature)","minAmountOut":"Minimum balance required before sweeping","preimageHash":"The preimage hash used at HTLC creation","sweepToken":"Token to sweep to the depositor (address(0) for ETH)","timelock":"Timelock set at HTLC creation","token":"ERC20 token locked in the HTLC"}},"executeAndCreateWithPermit2((address,uint256,bytes)[],bytes32,address,address,uint256,address,((address,uint256),uint256,uint256),bytes)":{"details":"The coordinator becomes the HTLC sender (depositor tracking enabled).      If the swap expires, only the depositor can call refundAndExecute;      refundTo is permissionless but always sends tokens to the depositor.","params":{"calls":"Arbitrary calls to execute (e.g. DEX swap)","claimAddress":"Address authorized to redeem the HTLC","depositor":"Address whose tokens are pulled via Permit2","permit":"Permit2 permit data (input token, amount, nonce, deadline)","preimageHash":"SHA-256 preimage hash for the HTLC","signature":"Permit2 signature from the depositor","timelock":"Unix timestamp after which a refund is possible","token":"Output ERC20 token to lock in the HTLC (e.g. WBTC after a USDC\u2192WBTC swap). collabRefundAndExecute(bytes32,uint256,a.details: Requires TWO signatures:      - Depositor EIP-712 sig (coordinator-level, authorizes refund params)      - ClaimAddress EIP-712 sig (HTLC-level, waives timelock via refundBySig)      Anyone can submit the transaction (gasless for both parties).

params.amount: Token amount locked in the HTLC
params.calls: Arbitrary calls to execute after refund (e.g. fee skim + DEX swap)
params.claimAddress: Claim address set at HTLC creation
params.claimR: ECDSA signature component (claimAddress's HTLC-level refundBySig signature)
params.claimS: ECDSA signature component (claimAddress's HTLC-level refundBySig signature)
params.claimV: ECDSA recovery id (claimAddress's HTLC-level refundBySig signature)
params.preimageHash: The preimage hash used at HTLC creation
params.sweepToken: Token to sweep to the depositor (address(0) for ETH)
params.token: ERC20 token locked in the HTLC

executeAndCreateWithPermit2((address,uin.details: The coordinator becomes the HTLC sender (depositor tracking enabled).      If the swap expires, only the depositor can call refundAndExecute;      refundTo is permissionless but always sends tokens to the depositor.

params.calls: Arbitrary calls to execute (e.g. DEX swap)
params.claimAddress: Address authorized to redeem the HTLC
params.depositor: Address whose tokens are pulled via Permit2
params.permit: Permit2 permit data (input token, amount, nonce, deadline)
params.preimageHash: SHA-256 preimage hash for the HTLC
params.timelock: Unix timestamp after which a refund is possible
params.token: Output ERC20 token to lock in the HTLC (e.g. WBTC after a USDC→WBTC swap).                     The input token is specified in permit.permitted.token.

redeemAndExecute(bytes32,uint256,address.details: The claimAddress signs an HTLC-level EIP-712 message authorizing this      coordinator as the caller and binding the destination address. Anyone can      submit the transaction, but the destination is cryptographically guaranteed      by the claimAddress's signature. If a malicious submitter changes the      destination, ecrecover returns the wrong address → swap key mismatch → revert.

params.amount: Token amount locked in the HTLC
params.calls: Arbitrary calls to execute after redeem (e.g. DEX swap)
params.destination: Address to receive swept tokens (signed by claimAddress)
params.preimage: Secret that SHA-256 hashes to the HTLC's preimageHash
params.sweepToken: Token to sweep to the destination (address(0) for ETH)
params.token: ERC20 token locked in the HTLC

refundAndExecute(bytes32,uint256,address.details: Restricted to the original depositor only. Arbitrary calls are executed      with the coordinator as msg.sender, so only the depositor should control      what calls are made (prevents token theft via malicious calls).      For permissionless refund without calls, use refundTo instead.

params.amount: Token amount locked in the HTLC
params.calls: Arbitrary calls to execute after refund (e.g. DEX swap-back)
params.claimAddress: Claim address set at HTLC creation
params.preimageHash: The preimage hash used at HTLC creation
params.sweepToken: Token to sweep to the depositor (address(0) for ETH)
params.token: ERC20 token locked in the HTLC

refundTo(bytes32,uint256,address,address.details: Permissionless — anyone can trigger this after timelock expiry.      Tokens always go to the depositor regardless of who calls.

params.amount: Token amount locked in the HTLC
params.claimAddress: Claim address set at HTLC creation
params.preimageHash: The preimage hash used at HTLC creation
params.token: ERC20 token locked in the HTLC

collabRefundAndExecute(bytes32,uint256,a.notice: Collaboratively refund a coordinator-created HTLC before timelock expiry,         execute arbitrary calls (e.g. fee skim + DEX swap), then sweep to depositor

executeAndCreateWithPermit2((address,uin.notice: Pull tokens from depositor via Permit2, execute arbitrary calls, then         lock the resulting balance in an HTLC with the coordinator as sender

redeemAndExecute(bytes32,uint256,address.notice: Redeem tokens from an HTLC via EIP-712 signature, execute arbitrary         calls, then sweep the resulting balance to a signed destination

refundAndExecute(bytes32,uint256,address.notice: Refund an expired HTLC created via this coordinator, execute arbitrary         calls (e.g. swap WBTC back to USDC), then sweep to the original depositor

refundTo(bytes32,uint256,address,address.notice: Refund an expired coordinator-created HTLC and send the locked         token directly to the original depositor (no swap-back)
